professional_persona: Mohammed Nasar, a 23‑year‑old driver, blends meticulous route planning and routine vehicle maintenance with a curiosity for local art, and they often share spontaneous smartphone photographs of roadside scenes while ensuring passengers reach their destinations safely and on time.
linguistic_persona: Mohammed Nasar is fluent in Malayalam, can read and write everyday texts, and possesses functional English sufficient to interpret traffic signs, GPS directions, and handle basic passenger requests, while having no formal knowledge of Hindi or other languages, and they occasionally practice listening to English tutorials online.
sports_persona: Mohammed Nasar, a cricket enthusiast, regularly plays matches at the village ground, follows the Kerala cricket team's Ranji Trophy games, and supports the Kerala Blasters in the Indian Super League, while occasionally joining friends for early‑morning jogs to stay fit.
arts_persona: Mohammed Nasar enjoys listening to soulful Mappila folk songs by K. J. Yesudas, watches Malayalam movies starring Mohanlal and Fahadh Faasil, and captures village life through his smartphone photography, inspired by the visual storytelling of cinematographer Santosh Sivan.
travel_persona: Mohammed Nasar prefers quick weekend getaways to nearby hill stations like Munnar and cultural towns such as Kozhikode, planning trips with GPS and a modest budget, while dreaming of exploring the historic forts of Hampi and the backwaters of Alleppey when his finances stabilize.
culinary_persona: Mohammed Nasar enjoys cooking Kerala biryani with aromatic basmati rice, ghee, and tender chicken, preparing soft appam using fermented rice batter and coconut milk, and relishes fish fry with raw mango chutney, often experimenting with mustard seeds and curry leaves for extra flavor.
persona: Mohammed Nasar, a curious 23‑year‑old driver, balances disciplined faith with a restless mind, often worrying over details yet finding calm in cricket, photography, and late‑night biryani cravings.
cultural_background: Mohammed Nasar is a 23‑year‑old Malayali Muslim from the rural outskirts of Malappuram, Kerala. He belongs to the Mappila community, which blends Arab‑influenced Islamic traditions with the vibrant Malayalam culture of South‑India. Growing up in a close‑knit village, he has been shaped by communal gatherings during Ramadan and Eid, as well as local festivals like Onam that celebrate harvest and art. His family environment emphasizes respect for elders, modesty, and a strong work ethic, which together with his curious nature give him an openness to new ideas and creative expressions.
linguistic_background: He grew up speaking Malayalam as his mother tongue and completed middle school education, which gave him functional literacy in Malayalam – he can read and write everyday texts, road signs and basic correspondence. For his driving job, he knows enough English to understand traffic signs, GPS instructions, and to communicate simple requests with occasional non‑Malayalam‑speaking passengers. He has no formal knowledge of Hindi or any other language, but his curiosity often leads him to listen to Malayalam songs and occasional YouTube tutorials in English.
skills_and_expertise: With several years behind the wheel, Mohammed has honed professional driving skills, including safe navigation of rural and urban roads, efficient route planning, and courteous customer interaction. He performs routine vehicle inspections, basic maintenance tasks like oil checks and tire pressure adjustments, and can troubleshoot minor mechanical issues. His familiarity with GPS and mobile payment apps ensures timely pickups and smooth cash handling. He also possesses good time‑management abilities, adapting to varying passenger schedules while maintaining reliability.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Professional Driving", "Safe Navigation of Rural and Urban Roads", "Route Planning", "Customer Service", "Vehicle Inspection", "Basic Vehicle Maintenance", "Minor Mechanical Troubleshooting", "GPS Navigation", "Mobile Payment Handling", "Time Management"]
hobbies_and_interests: Mohammed enjoys listening to traditional Mappila folk songs, watching Malayalam cinema, and playing cricket with friends in the village field. His curiosity about art leads him to capture roadside scenes with his smartphone camera and explore local craft fairs when they appear. He also likes cooking Kerala specialties like biryani and appam, and occasionally reads short stories in Malayalam to unwind after long shifts.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Mappila folk songs", "Watching Malayalam movies", "Playing cricket with friends", "Smartphone photography of roadside scenes", "Cooking Kerala dishes (biryani, appam)", "Reading Malayalam short stories", "Visiting local craft fairs"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Mohammed aspires to upgrade his livelihood by acquiring his own van and establishing a small transport service catering to local commuters and tourists in Malappuram. He aims to complete a certified driver‑training program to become an instructor, which would increase his earnings and give him a stable career path. Additionally, he wishes to save enough to support a future marriage and possibly pursue short courses in basic accounting and Malayalam literature, feeding his curiosity for art and learning while balancing his work responsibilities.
occupation: Car, Taxi and Van Drivers, Other
persona_clinical_summary: A 23-year-old male driver from rural Malappuram, Kerala, with middle education, who is never married. He speaks Malayalam and works in the transportation sector. His lifestyle includes regular cricket play and photography of local scenes.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every required entity from the doc-type profile at least once.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|482961570338]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Automated SMS Notifications for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~400 characters (2–4 sentences). Do not dump charts, labs, or long forms into an SMS.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 23
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Car, Taxi and Van Drivers, Other
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural
- state: Kerala
- district: Malappuram
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: A 23-year-old male driver from rural Malappuram, Kerala, with middle education, who is never married. He speaks Malayalam and works in the transportation sector. His lifestyle includes regular cricket play and photography of local scenes.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]].

Required inline entities (each ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]):
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

Optional entities (include when natural):
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]

Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text: [[PATIENT_NAME|Rohan Nair]]: നിങ്ങളുടെ ക്യാൻസർ ഫോളോ-അപ്പ് 15-നവംബർ രാവിലെ 11:00 മണിക്ക് [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Malappuram District Hospital]]-ൽ ആണ്. ദയവായി [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]-ൽ സ്ഥിരീകരിക്കുക. ഡോ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali]] നിങ്ങളെ പരിശോധിക്കും.
generated_text_en: [[PATIENT_NAME|Rohan Nair]]: Your oncology follow-up is on 15-Nov at 11:00 AM at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Malappuram District Hospital]]. Please confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali]] will see you.
judge_reasoning: Malayalam prose matches; allowed entities; no persona/domain mismatch; short SMS format; Latin tags expected.
